title: K.Prasanna Kumar vs Kantharaju on 25 April, 2018
body: K.Prasanna Kumar vs Kantharaju on 25 April, 2018 IN THE COURT OF LXVII ADDL CITY CIVIL AND SESSIONS JUDGE; BENGALURU CITY (CCH.No.68) PRESENT SRI.K.SUBRAMANYA, B.Com., LL.M. LXVII ADDL CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BENGALURU. Dated this the 25th day of April 2018. Crl. Appeal Nos.951/2016, APPELLANT/ACCUSED : K.Prasanna Kumar, (In all the cases) S/o.Y.K.Kambaiah, 48 years, R/of.Ananda Nilaya, Bairaveshwara Prasanna, No.115/2, 5th Link Road, Near Siddivinayaka Temple, Kathriguppe, Bengaluru. (By Sri.S.G.V., Advocate) .Vs. RESPONDENT/ Kantharaju, COMPLAINANT: S/o.Marimadaiah, (In C.C.Nos.5918/2013 35 years, & 5919/2013) R/at.No.15/7, 22nd Cross, (Opp. Rathna Medicals) K.P.Agrahara, Bhuvaneshwarinagar, Bengaluru. (In C.C.No.5920/2013) Smt.Jayamma, W/o.S.D.Manjunath, 45 years, R/at.No.53, I Main, 2nd Cross, Bhuvaneshwarinagar, Bengaluru. 2 Crl.Appeal No.951/2016 (In C.C.No.5921/2013) Smt.Kamalakumari C. W/o.S.R.Nagaraju, 43 years, R/at.No.1051, 14th Main, Srinagar, Bengaluru. (By Sri.V.M., Advocate) JUDGMENT This appeal is preferred by the appellant under Section 374(3) of C.P.C., challenging the conviction judgment passed by the learned XVIII Addl. C.M.M., Bengaluru in C.C.Nos.5918/2013, 5919/2013, 5920/2013 and 5921/2013, dated:28.07.2016. 2. Brief facts of the case are as under: The complainants/respondents have filed private complaint under Section 200 of Cr.P.C., for the alleged offence under Section 138 of N.I.Act. It is stated in the complaint that the accused/appellant is known to the complainants/respondents for past several years and promised to open BPO Share Trading Business. The accused availed the loan and to discharge the same, issued the cheques and the said cheques came to be dishonoured. Hence, the cases are filed and the impugned order of conviction has been passed and the same has been challenged in this appeal. 3. In the grounds of appeal, the appellant has contended that the conclusion arrived is erroneous. The trial court has not considered the defense. The trial court has not at all 3 Crl.Appeal No.951/2016 considered the legal fiction under Section 138 of N.I.Act. The trial court has failed to consider about the existence of legally enforceable debt. The impugned order passed merely on admitting that the cheques belongs to the accused/appellant. The defense as to mis-use of cheques, which were lost are not at all considered. The appellant have adduced same evidence and got acquittal from the other cases, wherein family members of the complainant filed the cases. Hence, sought for allowing the appeal by setting aside the impugned order. 4. The respondents have put their appearance through their counsel. The lower court records were secured. 5. After hearing the arguments, the points raised for determination are as under: 1. Whether the appellant has made out grounds to intermeddle with the impugned order ? 2. What Order ? 6. My findings on the above points are as follows. POINT No.1 - In the Negative. POINT No.2 - As per final order, for the following : REASONS 7. POINT No.1: The trial court after recording the evidence of all the cases and prima-facie, looking into the prime documents-cheques and bank endorsements and the 4 Crl.Appeal No.951/2016 defense documents has come to the conclusion that the accused has not adduced rebuttal evidence to rebut the presumption raised under Section 138 of N.I.Act. The cheques issued for existence of debt on even dates for Rs.1,00,000/- and Rs.1,00,000/- each in clubbed cases are clearly appreciated by the trial court adverting to the cumulative effect of evidence adduced by both the parties. 8. The parties are involved in share trading business and in that regard, the cheques are issued. The cheques are issued during March/April 2012 are clearly establishing that the cheques were endorsed in favour of the complainant and it has been dishonoured for "Funds Insufficient". 9. In the course of evidence and trial, the accused contended that the cheques were stolen as the cheque book is kept in the vehicle and it has been mis-used. It is also stated that he has lodged the complaint and given paper publication. On meticulously perusing the defense, the varied date of complaint and delay in making paper publication at the time of filing the case does not in any way come to the aid of the defense. It is the contention of the defense that the cheque book has been stolen, but he has stated that few cheques were signed and kept blank in the vehicle, as there is no security in the house. It is to be noted that the moving vehicle in unsecured place they were kept, how the complainant came to know that it is in the 5 Crl.Appeal No.951/2016 vehicle and how he has stolen the same is not established in defense. The criminal case registered and proved against the complainant as to stealing of cheque book is not forthcoming. There is an inordinate delay in paper publication and the complaint as to missing of cheque books. Therefore, the defense is not in any way reliable and accordingly, the trial court has come to the conclusion that there is no rebuttal evidence in view of due endorsement of the holder of the cheque is having cheque in its course. In that view of the admission as to execution of cheque, the defense is not in any way reliable. It is also observed in the impugned judgment that to disprove the contention of the complainant, the accused has not produced his bank account extract or passbook. If he is intimated to the bank as to the theft and lost of passbook, then the endorsement would have been different rather than dishonour for "Funds Insufficient". Hence, an after - thought that to put forth the defense is not in any way justified or appreciated. 10. The trial court also observed in para No.13 of the judgment all these factors and disputes made and the presumption and inference drawn under Section 114(g) of Indian Evidence Act. The bank account extract with-held deliberately raised an adverse inference against the accused. Hence, the appeal is devoid of merits. The impugned order deserves to be confirmed in view of presumption under Sections 118 and 139 of N.I.Act and the 6 Crl.Appeal No.951/2016 onus of rebuttal evidence has not been discharged by the accused in defense evidence. The trial court also relied upon the dictum and the principles observed in AIR 2010 SC 1989 (Rangappa .Vs. Mohan). Hence, the appeal is dismissed. Accordingly, I answer the Point No.1 in the Negative. 11. POINT No.2 : My finding on this point is as per following : ORDER The Crl. Appeal filed by the appellant is hereby dismissed. Consequently, the impugned judgment passed by the learned XVIII Addl. C.M.M., Bengaluru in C.C.Nos.5918/2013, 5919/2013, 5920/2013 and 5921/2013, dated:28.07.2016 stands confirmed. Send back the records to the lower court along with the copy of this judgment. (Dictated to the Judgment-writer, transcript thereof is corrected and then pronounced by me in the open court on this the 25th day of April 2018) (K.SUBRAMANYA) LXVII Addl.City Civil and Sessions Judge, BENGALURU. Digitally signed by KRISHNAMURTHY SUBRAMANYA KRISHNAMURTHY DN: cn=KRISHNAMURTHY SUBRAMANYA,o=GOVERNMENT SUBRAMANYA OF KARNATAKA,st=Karnataka,c=IN Date: 2018.04.27 11:56:58 IST
